description:
You are a deployment engineer who has just finished building the v2 release of your company’s web-service.  
    A colleague prepared the updated code under

        /home/user/releases/v2/

    Before the release can be shipped you must package it, copy it to the deployment area, unpack it in a
    staging directory, create a checksum manifest, verify the integrity of the unpacked files and store
    a verification log.

    EXISTING SOURCE TREE (already present when the session starts)
    └── /home/user/releases/v2
        ├── README.md
        ├── app.conf
        └── scripts
            └── start.sh

    Detailed requirements

    1. Create a compressed tarball called               app_update_v2.tar.gz
       • It must contain exactly the three files shown above.  
       • Inside the archive those files must live under a single top-level directory named
           app_update_v2
         so that, for example, the README ends up in
           app_update_v2/README.md
       • Save the archive in /home/user/deployments (create that directory if it does not exist).

    2. Unpack the archive in a **new** directory:

           /home/user/deployments/staging

       After extraction the resulting tree must look like

           /home/user/deployments/staging/app_update_v2/README.md
           /home/user/deployments/staging/app_update_v2/app.conf
           /home/user/deployments/staging/app_update_v2/scripts/start.sh

    3. Inside /home/user/deployments/staging create a file named

           checksums.sha256

       The file must list the SHA-256 hash of each file (not directory) inside
       app_update_v2.  Use exactly the output format produced by the command

           sha256sum  <relative_path>

       i.e. each line must be  
           <64-character-hash><two spaces><relative_path>

       with <relative_path> being relative to the directory where the manifest lies.
       Example line (hash will differ):

           e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e...  app_update_v2/README.md

       The order of the three lines is not important.

    4. Still inside /home/user/deployments/staging verify the manifest with sha256sum and capture
       ONLY the verification output into a log file named

           verification.log

       Required log-file format (exactly three lines, each terminated with a line-feed):

           app_update_v2/README.md: OK
           app_update_v2/app.conf: OK
           app_update_v2/scripts/start.sh: OK

       No extra whitespace is allowed; the case must match exactly.

    5. Remove any copy of app_update_v2.tar.gz from the **staging** directory
       (the copy in /home/user/deployments must remain).

    Summary of artefacts expected when you finish:

        /home/user/deployments/app_update_v2.tar.gz          (archive exists)
        /home/user/deployments/staging/app_update_v2/...     (three unpacked files)
        /home/user/deployments/staging/checksums.sha256      (3-line manifest)
        /home/user/deployments/staging/verification.log      (exactly as specified)

    Use any valid series of 5–10 shell commands to accomplish the task.
truth:
<!--  Pre-existing files and their exact contents  -->
    <pre_existing>
        <file path="/home/user/releases/v2/README.md"><![CDATA[# App v2 Update
This release fixes bugs and improves performance.
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/releases/v2/app.conf"><![CDATA[version=2.0.1
environment=production
debug=false
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/releases/v2/scripts/start.sh"><![CDATA[#!/bin/bash
echo "Starting App v2..."
]]></file>
    </pre_existing>

    <!--  Expected final verification log  -->
    <expected_log path="/home/user/deployments/staging/verification.log"><![CDATA[app_update_v2/README.md: OK
app_update_v2/app.conf: OK
app_update_v2/scripts/start.sh: OK
]]></expected_log>

    <!--  Additional expectations  -->
    <expected_files>
        <file path="/home/user/deployments/app_update_v2.tar.gz">TARBALL</file>
        <file path="/home/user/deployments/staging/checksums.sha256">MANIFEST</file>
    </expected_files>
